headline: Harper and Sanchez turn the rebound into a streak
summary_text: The Phillies' week finally has traction: after Aaron Nola and Bryce Harper squeezed out a 1-0 win in Miami, Harper homered twice and Cristopher Sanchez worked eight shutout innings in a 9-1 opener against the Athletics. That makes it three straight wins and pushes the club to 16-20, still fourth in the NL East but playing with a clearer formula than it had during the skid: starting pitching is carrying the run prevention, Harper is punishing mistakes, and the bullpen is settling into more defined roles. Wednesday's follow-up with Zack Wheeler against Jeffrey Springs is a good test of whether this is just a soft landing or the start of a real climb.
